summary: Verified two new content additions: (1) `## Common Questions` block added to /app/frontend/llms.txt AFTER `## Key Facts` with the 7 verbatim Q&A bullets in exact order; (2) NEW auto-generated /app/frontend/llms-full.txt emitted by build-static.js (5295 bytes) with H1 + blockquote intro + Common Questions block + 6 top-level route sections (home titled `## California Cooperage`, others by h1) each with `URL:` line + `### Specifications` on CR1/CR2/CR3 (12-14 rows each) + `### Frequently Asked Questions` on home + 3 product pages + fully-decoded entities + zero HTML tag leakage; (3) _headers now has 17 blocks (was 16) with new /llms-full.txt block Cache-Control 1h + Content-Type text/plain; charset=utf-8. Created /app/backend/tests/test_llms_files.py (62 tests). Bumped 16→17 header-block-count assertions in 5 pre-existing files. Adjusted stale llms.txt size range (1500-2200 → 2100-2700) since Common Questions added ~500 bytes. Full suite: 394/394 pass. Live preview verified: /llms-full.txt returns 200 + text/plain; charset=utf-8; body starts with '# California Cooperage — Full Content' and contains all 7 Common Questions. Idempotency confirmed: 2 consecutive `node build-static.js` invocations produce identical md5 for llms-full.txt AND leave llms.txt untouched (source file preserved).
critical_code_review_comments: The 17-block count assertion is now duplicated across 6 test files (test_headers_file, test_seo_markup_fixes, test_content_updates, test_pid_urls, test_heater_spec_updates, test_meta_descriptions_and_llms, test_llms_files). Recommend factoring into a shared conftest fixture (e.g., EXPECTED_HEADERS_BLOCK_COUNT constant in /app/backend/tests/conftest.py) so future header additions require touching only one file.

Home section in llms-full.txt is titled `## California Cooperage` (per review spec) rather than the h1 heading `California's Original Hot Tub`. Intentional per the review request but worth flagging — it means this one section deviates from the 'others by their h1 heading' rule.

Only home + CR1/CR2/CR3 have `### Frequently Asked Questions` in llms-full.txt output. Warranty and Find-a-Dealer route sections do NOT contain FAQs. Review request said 'warranty possibly' — current impl skips warranty FAQs; test tolerates this (only requires FAQs on home+cr1+cr2+cr3).
llms-full.txt is emitted as an idempotent build artifact (5295 bytes, stable md5 across builds). llms.txt remains a source file (build never touches it). Clean separation of concerns.

No HTML entity or tag leakage detected — extractSpecTable() and decodeEntities() helpers work correctly (" → ", no
//
etc).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_meta_descriptions_and_llms.py (16→17 header block count; llms.txt size range 1500-2200 → 2100-2700 for Common Questions)
/app/backend/tests/test_headers_file.py (16→17 block count + docstring + expected list updated for /llms-full.txt)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full backend regression is green (394 tests across 9 files). New file test_llms_files.py covers the Common Questions addition (llms.txt) + entire llms-full.txt shape (route sections, spec tables, FAQs, entity decoding, HTML tag guard, live preview, idempotency). Header block count moved 16 → 17; the assertion is now duplicated across 6 test files — future _headers changes should either update all 6 references or the count should be consolidated into a conftest fixture. llms-full.txt is a BUILD ARTIFACT (auto-generated by node build-static.js); llms.txt remains a hand-edited SOURCE FILE that the build must never modify. Both idempotency invariants are enforced in test_llms_files.py::TestBuildIdempotency.
